solutionModules.label: GCC lab Tender Lead AI Department
solutionModules.summary: Review GCClab CRM Lead cases and tender files, assess fit, match existing suppliers or partners, discover new suppliers when needed, request quotations, prepare tender packages, and follow up until submission or closure.
modulesUsing: GCClab CRM / Blue Mark Business Sphere
solutionModules.operatingRule: Lead -> tender document review -> fit/eligibility -> supplier/partner match -> quotation outreach -> tender package -> follow-up -> submit / close.

solutionModules.label: Sample Sales Research Department
solutionModules.summary: Reusable sample department template for lead discovery, fit review, and CRM follow-up.
solutionModules.operatingRule: Department -> Capability -> Playbook -> Work Step -> Tool Lane -> QA Gate -> Output.

solutionModules.label: SEO Demand Engine Department
solutionModules.summary: Use transcript/source inputs, Search Console and Analytics data, case studies, AI content generation, internal linking, WordPress publishing, and post-publish learning to run an SEO content demand engine.
solutionModules.operatingRule: Source text/data -> keyword evidence -> opportunity strategy -> case study mapping -> brief -> article drafts -> QA -> approval -> WordPress/Make.com publish -> performance learning.

capabilities.label: Tender Lead Intake And Document Review
capabilities.summary: Receive Lead records and files from GCClab CRM, read tender PDFs/specs/forms, extract scope, deadlines, eligibility clauses, and first blockers.

capabilities.label: Fit, Eligibility And Supplier Match
capabilities.summary: Check whether GCC lab can bid directly, needs a partner, or needs supplier quotations; match Companies, Contacts, Products_Registered, LeadMatchedSuppliers, and vendor lists.

capabilities.label: Supplier Discovery And Mapping
capabilities.summary: When no suitable supplier or partner exists in GCC lab data, find ideal suppliers, map contacts, and prepare evidence-backed candidates for outreach.

capabilities.label: Tender Package Preparation
capabilities.summary: Prepare compliance matrices, required tender forms, technical response, commercial summary, supplier quotation evidence, and submission checklist.

capabilities.label: Supplier Quotation Outreach
capabilities.summary: Ask selected suppliers whether they are interested and request price, technical confirmation, lead time, validity, exclusions, and required quote evidence.

capabilities.label: Supplier And Client Follow-up
capabilities.summary: Track supplier quotation replies, tender Q&A, client or tender-owner clarifications, and post-submission response status.

capabilities.label: GCClab CRM Case Sync
capabilities.summary: Keep local task threads aligned with GCClab CRM tables when CRM sync is enabled, especially Lead, Tasks List, Companies, Contacts, LeadMatchedSuppliers, and LeadMatchedProducts.

capabilities.label: Tender Workflow Automation
capabilities.summary: Run lead/case review cycles until the next action is blocked by missing tender files, supplier quote, human decision, or CRM transfer.

capabilities.label: Manager Routing And AI Reasoning
capabilities.summary: Interpret human instructions and transferred Lead payloads, route work to the correct AI staff, and keep supplier/client communication gated.

capabilities.label: SEO Source And Transcript Intake
capabilities.summary: Read pasted transcripts, lessons, briefs, or strategy notes and convert them into structured department requirements, workflow steps, and source evidence.

capabilities.label: Search Console And Analytics Review
capabilities.summary: Read Google Search Console and Analytics data by website, keyword, page, impressions, clicks, ranking movement, and growth trajectory.

capabilities.label: Keyword Opportunity Strategy
capabilities.summary: Find reoptimization and new-content opportunities based on what each website already partially ranks for, not generic keyword-volume guesses.

capabilities.label: Case Study And Unique Evidence Mapping
capabilities.summary: Match target keywords to company case studies, first-hand experience, proof, examples, and unique data so content is not generic.

capabilities.label: SEO Brief And Article Draft Generation
capabilities.summary: Generate content briefs and five article drafts per approved keyword across one or more websites, using matched case studies and SEO metadata.

capabilities.label: Internal Link Planning
capabilities.summary: Suggest relevant internal links and anchor text from the site inventory before content is approved for publishing.

capabilities.label: Supervised WordPress And Make.com Publishing
capabilities.summary: Prepare WordPress publishing payloads or Make.com scenario triggers, but hold external publishing until Iman approves.

capabilities.label: SEO Performance Learning Loop
capabilities.summary: Track published URLs, keyword movement, clicks, impressions, and lessons for the next content round.

recipes.label: Intake And Review Tender Lead
recipes.summary: Receive a Lead and files, read tender documents, extract scope/deadlines/requirements, and hand off to Leo for fit and supplier matching.

recipes.label: Assess Tender Fit And Supplier Route
recipes.summary: Score GCC lab fit, compliance, deadline, partner need, and existing supplier/partner match; decide direct bid, partner bid, quote route, or no-bid.

recipes.label: Map New Suppliers
recipes.summary: Find and evidence new suppliers when GCC lab has no suitable existing supplier or partner.

recipes.label: Prepare Tender Submission Package
recipes.summary: Build compliance matrix, technical response, commercial summary, required forms, quotation evidence, and submission checklist.

recipes.label: Request Supplier Quotations Safely
recipes.summary: Prepare approved supplier quotation requests and collect interest, pricing, delivery, validity, and exclusions.

recipes.label: Follow Up Supplier And Tender Replies
recipes.summary: Reconcile replies to Lead, supplier/contact, Q&A, quotation, or submission status and create due follow-ups.

recipes.label: Run GCClab CRM Case Health Cycle
recipes.summary: Audit local/CRM lead case links, supplier matches, quote status, missing files, and stale tasks.

recipes.label: Run Tender Case KPI Cycle
recipes.summary: Advance tender lead review, supplier matching, quotation follow-up, and package readiness until blocked or complete.

recipes.label: Interpret And Route Manager Thread
recipes.summary: Use the Manager AI brain to interpret natural human messages and route work safely.

recipes.label: Read Source Text And Extract Department Requirements
recipes.summary: Use pasted transcripts and notes to identify workflow, staff, tools, datasets, outputs, and gates.

recipes.label: Analyze Search Performance By Website
recipes.summary: Load website/search data and prepare keyword/page performance views.

recipes.label: Rank Reoptimization And New Content Opportunities
recipes.summary: Use existing rank/impression/click patterns to decide what to reoptimize or create next.

recipes.label: Map Case Studies To Keywords
recipes.summary: Select relevant first-hand proof and data for each keyword/content angle.

recipes.label: Generate SEO Briefs And Article Drafts
recipes.summary: Create briefs and multiple article drafts from keyword plus case study evidence.

recipes.label: Build Internal Link Plan
recipes.summary: Add relevant internal links and anchors before publishing.

recipes.label: Prepare And Publish With Approval
recipes.summary: Prepare WordPress/Make.com payloads and block until human approval.

recipes.label: Track SEO Performance And Learn
recipes.summary: Track rankings/clicks/impressions after publishing and create next-round learning.

aiSupport.label: Manager low-cost reasoning
aiSupport.usage: Interpret human messages, classify intent, and route work.

aiSupport.label: Research reasoning
aiSupport.usage: Tender Case search planning, source assessment, and supplier-fit synthesis.

aiSupport.label: Document writing
aiSupport.usage: tender response/compliance quality and supplier-specific framing.

aiSupport.label: Outreach safety reasoning
aiSupport.usage: Check outbound wording for unsafe internal/system language.

aiSupport.label: SEO strategy reasoning
aiSupport.usage: Cluster keywords, identify opportunity patterns, classify intent, and prioritize reoptimization/new-page work.

aiSupport.label: SEO content writing
aiSupport.usage: Create structured content briefs and article drafts with case-study-specific evidence.

qualityGates.label: Tender / Supplier Evidence
qualityGates.rule: Tender facts, supplier matches, vendor status, and portal details must cite tender files, official source, GCClab CRM table, or supplier evidence.

qualityGates.label: No Duplicate Lead Case
qualityGates.rule: Do not create duplicate Lead-case work; update the existing Lead, task, or thread instead.

qualityGates.label: Compliance Requirements
qualityGates.rule: Mandatory tender eligibility, registration, certificate, technical, commercial, and legal requirements must be extracted and assigned.

qualityGates.label: Tender Deadline Check
qualityGates.rule: Closing date, Q&A deadline, job date, and urgent risks must be known or explicitly marked missing.

qualityGates.label: GCC Capability Fit
qualityGates.rule: Leo must score whether GCC lab can bid directly, needs a partner, needs supplier quotes, or should no-bid.

qualityGates.label: Supplier Source Evidence
qualityGates.rule: Nadia supplier candidates must cite company website, registry, verified vendor list, LinkedIn/company evidence, or CRM evidence.

qualityGates.label: Source Freshness
qualityGates.rule: Current tender case/scope/deadline facts must be verified against current sources.

qualityGates.label: Document Template Style
qualityGates.rule: Generated docs must use the agreed Harvard/academic/tender response templates, not plain placeholder formatting.

qualityGates.label: Tender Specificity
qualityGates.rule: Tender package and supplier outreach must refer to the actual Lead, tender scope, requirements, and supplier quote needs.

qualityGates.label: Tender Files Registered
qualityGates.rule: Every active tender case needs transferred Lead files or explicit missing-file blocker.

qualityGates.label: Content Safety
qualityGates.rule: Outbound supplier outreach cannot mention internal failures, package archive problems, or system instructions.

qualityGates.label: Tender Package Completeness
qualityGates.rule: Tender package needs compliance matrix, forms, technical response, commercial summary, supplier quotes, and submission checklist or explicit blockers.

qualityGates.label: Tender File Access
qualityGates.rule: Transferred files and supplier quote evidence must be accessible before package preparation or submission.

qualityGates.label: Duplicate Supplier Recipient
qualityGates.rule: Repeated supplier/contact outreach requires review unless it is a planned follow-up in the same case.

qualityGates.label: Reply Match Confidence
qualityGates.rule: Replies must match by Lead, supplier/contact, thread, email domain, or tender reference before status updates.

qualityGates.label: Follow-up Due Date
qualityGates.rule: Every waiting supplier/client/tender-owner action needs a due follow-up unless closed or submitted.

qualityGates.label: Sync Error Check
qualityGates.rule: Sync/bridge errors become Technical Bug or System Audit tasks, not hidden logs.

qualityGates.label: Active Entity Follow-up
qualityGates.rule: Every active AI-owned entity needs an active, not-overdue follow-up or queued task.

qualityGates.label: Event Log Trace
qualityGates.rule: Important status/responsible/stage changes need an event-log or thread record.

qualityGates.label: KPI Progress
qualityGates.rule: The daily/weekly cycle continues until KPI targets are met or a blocker is explicit.

qualityGates.label: No Hidden Blocker
qualityGates.rule: When the cycle stops without work, Manager asks Human for a plan instead of silently stopping.

qualityGates.label: No Outreach From Chat
qualityGates.rule: Thread replies never send email by themselves.

qualityGates.label: Safe Routing
qualityGates.rule: Specialist staff talk to Manager first; only Manager talks to Human.

qualityGates.label: Source Text Extracted
qualityGates.rule: Transcript/text inputs must be converted into clear workflow steps, tools, datasets, staff needs, and approval gates before department design work continues.

qualityGates.label: Search Console Connected
qualityGates.rule: Keyword opportunity work cannot be treated as data-backed until Search Console property access or a reviewed export is available.

qualityGates.label: Keyword Evidence Required
qualityGates.rule: Every recommended keyword must cite Search Console/Analytics evidence, current ranking/impressions/clicks, or a clearly marked manual source.

qualityGates.label: Case Study Specificity
qualityGates.rule: Content drafts must include a matched first-hand case study, company proof, data point, or unique insight; generic filler is blocked.

qualityGates.label: Non-Generic Content
qualityGates.rule: Drafts must include useful, original, entity-specific information and cannot be only generic SEO text.

qualityGates.label: SEO Metadata Check
qualityGates.rule: Drafts require SEO title target 50-60 characters, meta description/excerpt target 140-160 characters with no quotes, one primary long-tail keyword, 3-8 supporting keywords, suggested URL slug, clean WordPress HTML, H2/H3 headings, short paragraphs, key takeaways, nofollow external references, categories[] with no more than one category, tags[], and a final checklist before any Make.com webhook action.

qualityGates.label: Internal Links Check
qualityGates.rule: Internal link suggestions must point to existing relevant pages or be marked as missing inventory.

qualityGates.label: Publish Approval Required
qualityGates.rule: WordPress publishing, Make.com scenario triggering, and external site changes require Iman approval. Always prepare draft status unless Iman explicitly says publish, and ask for confirmation before calling the Make.com webhook action.

qualityGates.label: Published URL Logged
qualityGates.rule: Every published article must log URL, keyword, website, date, approval, and source draft before performance tracking starts.

operatingNotes.workMap:
# Work Map

The GCC lab AI department moves tender work from `Lead` intake to tender document review, bid/no-bid, supplier or partner matching, quotation outreach, tender package preparation, and follow-up.

## Ava - Tender Document Analyst
Reads Lead details, tender PDFs, specs, forms, BOQs, deadlines, and source portals.

## Leo - Fit & Supplier Match Analyst
Checks GCC lab capability fit, tender requirements, Companies, Contacts, Products_Registered, LeadMatchedSuppliers, LeadMatchedProducts, and verified vendor lists.

## Nadia - Supplier Mapper
Finds ideal new suppliers when existing GCC lab supplier/partner data is not enough.

## Omar - Supplier Outreach Specialist
Requests supplier interest, quotations, pricing, delivery terms, and technical confirmation after approval.

## Maya - Tender Package Maker
Prepares compliance matrix, forms, technical response, commercial summary, supplier quotation evidence, and submission checklist.

## Lina - Follow-up Controller
Tracks supplier replies, Q&A deadlines, quotation gaps, client/tender-owner responses, and post-submission follow-up.

## Noah - CRM Controller
Maintains local/CRM integrity for Lead, Companies, Contacts, Tasks List, LeadMatchedSuppliers, LeadMatchedProducts, and related tables.
operatingNotes.lanesTools:
# Lanes & Tools

- `GCClab CRM`: source of truth for `Lead`, `Companies`, `Contacts`, `Tasks List`, `LeadMatchedSuppliers`, `LeadMatchedProducts`, `Products_Registered`, `Orders`, and supporting tables.
- `Tender Document Files`: transferred PDFs/forms/specs/BOQs attached to the Lead case.
- `Official Web Sources`: tender portals, vendor registries, supplier websites, official company pages.
- `Supplier Outreach Channel`: email/communications for approved quotation requests.
- `Local Task Threads`: local-first discussion and decision memory while CRM sync is disabled.
operatingNotes.qualityGates:
# Quality Gates

Core blocking gates: Lead Record Complete, Tender Documents Available, Tender Deadline Check, GCC Capability Fit, Compliance Requirements, Bid / No-Bid Decision, Supplier Match Evidence, Supplier Outreach Approval, Pricing Evidence, Tender Package Completeness, Follow-up Due Date, Sync Error Check, No Outreach From Chat, Safe Routing.
operatingNotes.dataConnectors:
# Data & Connectors

## Core GCClab CRM Tables
- `Lead`: prepared active commercial pipeline. Key: `Lead ID`; label: `Lead Name`; status: `Lead Status`.
- `BusinessOpportunities`: early/non-prepared tender case intake. Key: `BizOpp ID`.
- `Companies`: company master for suppliers, active suppliers, clients, partners. Key: `ID`; label: `Name`.
- `Contacts`: contact records. Key: `Corporate Emails`.
- `Tasks List`: delegated CRM tasks and follow-ups. Key: `ID`; `OwningDepartment` is required for API create.
- `LeadMatchedSuppliers`: supplier matches per lead.
- `LeadMatchedProducts`: product matches per lead.
- `Products_Registered`: products/services registered by company.
- `LeadExtractedProducts_Request` / `LeadExtractedProducts_Result`: extracted product requirements from lead/tender files.
- `EmailsLog`: communication log.
- `Orders`: purchase order table after a won Lead.

CRM sync is optional in this local command center. When disabled, the department works local-first and waits for a future action to transfer Lead information and files.
operatingNotes.aiBrain:
# AI Brain

Manager routes each Lead case. Ava reads tender details and documents. Leo evaluates fit and existing supplier/partner matches. Nadia maps new suppliers. Omar handles approved supplier quotation outreach. Maya prepares tender response packages. Lina tracks follow-ups. Noah maintains CRM/local integrity.
operatingNotes.outputTemplates:
# Output Templates

- Lead Case Brief
- Tender Requirement Matrix
- Supplier / Partner Shortlist
- New Supplier Contact Map
- Quotation Request Log
- Tender Submission Package
- Manager Operating Report
operatingNotes.learningLibrary:
# Learning Library

Closed Manager/Human threads can become tender-department learning candidates. Approved learnings can update staff role files for Ava, Leo, Nadia, Omar, Maya, Lina, Noah, or Manager routing rules.

departments.approvalPolicy: Publishing to WordPress, triggering Make.com scenarios, connecting external accounts, and applying learned SEO rules require Iman approval.

goals.label: Publish SEO content
goals.description: Create approved, SEO-ready WordPress draft articles and publish only after Iman confirms the Make.com action.
goals.progressSource: Manual/local alpha until WordPress publish log is connected
goals.nextAction: Prepare SEO article packages, request Iman approval, then send approved drafts through the Make.com WordPress publisher.

google_search_console.configurationNotes: WorldBusiness Council / Worldbc.co Search Console property. Confirm the exact Search Console property value; Domain properties usually use sc-domain:worldbc.co.
google_search_console.lastTestMessage: Organization configuration check passed. OAuth can be connected when you are ready.

info.title: WorldBC WordPress Publisher (via Make Webhook)
info.description: Creates a WordPress blog post draft by sending optimized article content to a Make.com webhook. The Make scenario handles publishing to WordPress.

post.description: Sends article title, HTML content, SEO excerpt, categories, and tags to Make.com, which creates a WordPress post. Default status is draft.

content.description: Full article body in clean WordPress HTML.

categories.description: Exactly one WordPress category ID unless user provides a supported alternative.

editorialPolicy.role: Editorial + SEO assistant for a WordPress blog
editorialPolicy.tone: Investopedia.com style: professional, clear, credible, and easy to understand.
editorialPolicy.publishingRule: Always publish as draft unless Iman explicitly says publish.
requiredPrePublishOutputs: Meta description / excerpt, target 140-160 characters, no quotes
Primary long-tail keyword, specific and intent-based
categories[] with no more than one category ID
articleRequirements: Use structured H2/H3 headings; H1 is title only and not repeated in body
Use short paragraphs and bullets where useful
Include a short Key takeaways section at the end
Include external links to famous/reputable resources in text as [1], [2]
Provide reference list after Key takeaways
seoRules: Pick a specific long-tail keyword matching user intent, audience, outcome, and context
Use the primary keyword naturally in title if possible, first 100 words, one H2, conclusion, and excerpt if natural
editorialPolicy.categoryPolicy: If categories/tags are not provided by the user, use the category knowledge-base file to select the ideal category ID. Never use more than one category.

make_com_webhook.referencePolicy: External reputable links must appear in text as [1], [2]; reference list after Key takeaways; links use rel="nofollow".
make_com_webhook.categorySelectionPolicy: If categories/tags are not provided by the user, use the category knowledge-base file to select the ideal category ID. Never use more than one category.
make_com_webhook.configurationNotes: WorldBusiness Council Make.com scenario for supervised WordPress draft publishing. The webhook URL is passed as secure runtime input from the local environment, not stored in the catalog. Do not call automatically. Prepare the full SEO/article checklist and ask Iman for confirmation before calling createWordpressPost.
make_com_webhook.lastTestMessage: Organization webhook configuration check passed. Webhook action remains blocked until Iman explicitly confirms.

kpis.label: Quote Follow-up Compliance
kpis.minimumEvidenceLevel: No waiting supplier/client action without due follow-up

kpis.label: Tender Leads Reviewed
kpis.minimumEvidenceLevel: Lead record + tender source/file evidence required

kpis.label: Tender Packages Ready
kpis.minimumEvidenceLevel: Compliance matrix + quote evidence + QA gates passed

kpis.label: Follow-up Compliance
kpis.minimumEvidenceLevel: No active AI-owned entity without due follow-up

reportDefinitions.label: Manager Operating Report
reportDefinitions.purpose: Explain what is happening, why it matters, and the next action.

reportDefinitions.label: Department Health
reportDefinitions.purpose: Show sync health, stale work, blockers, and backup/version state.

lockedPlatformRules: Thread replies never send email by themselves.
Only AI Manager creates human-facing tasks.
External sends require queue approval, content safety, package completeness, and attachment verification.
workspaceEditableRules: Tool assignments and non-locked QA gates
governance.versioning: Every designer save writes a Department Version row.
governance.backupPolicy: Create a local backup before major config changes.
governance.learningPolicy: Approved human learning is added only after explicit approval.

staffArchetypes.label: Manager / Orchestrator
staffArchetypes.purpose: Receive human intent, select the right specialist, create or route tasks, and keep the department moving without bypassing safety gates.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: fast reasoning model for routing; higher reasoning only for complex strategy
staffArchetypes.whenToUse: Receive human intent, select the right specialist, create or route tasks, and keep the department moving without bypassing safety gates.
staffArchetypes.defaultStageResponsibility: Execute assigned workflow stages under the AI Manager and return structured evidence.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: Route to local task/thread lane and ask AI Manager when unavailable.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Resolve by active workspace connection; Gmail/Outlook/API provider stays in lane adapter.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: Route to local task/thread lane and ask AI Manager when unavailable.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Resolve by active workspace connection; Gmail/Outlook/API provider stays in lane adapter.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: Route to local task/thread lane and ask AI Manager when unavailable.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Resolve by active workspace connection; Gmail/Outlook/API provider stays in lane adapter.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: Route to local task/thread lane and ask AI Manager when unavailable.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Resolve by active workspace connection; Gmail/Outlook/API provider stays in lane adapter.

staffArchetypes.missingDataBehavior: Ask AI Manager to create a human-facing missing-input thread; do not contact the human directly.

instructionLayers.summary: Platform safety, manager-facing routing, and protected execution rules.
Department vocabulary, approval policy, and output preferences within policy.
Reusable job-description defaults for this staff template.
Safe tone, sender, identity, dataset, and naming preferences.
Proposed from closed threads and activated only after approval.

inputContract.missingInputAction: Ask AI Manager to request the missing input.

outputContractDetail.approvalRequirement: Manager review; human approval for external or irreversible action.

forbiddenBehavior: do not execute external sends without approval

retryPolicy.askManagerWhen: confidence is low, provider unavailable, or task should be rerouted
retryPolicy.askHumanWhen: required business input or approval is missing
retryPolicy.stopStageWhen: guardrail fails or non-retryable blocker is detected
retryPolicy.createThreadWhen: approval, missing input, review, escalation, or real blocker exists

staffArchetypes.label: Thinking / Analysis Model
staffArchetypes.purpose: Read evidence, reason over fit and risk, produce structured recommendations, and cite sources before routing downstream.

staffArchetypes.label: Email Sender / Outreach Operator
staffArchetypes.purpose: Prepare, safety-check, queue, and process approved outbound emails through configured mail plugins or local SMTP draft mode.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: low-cost model for safety checks; no creative rewriting after approval without review

staffArchetypes.label: Document / Package Maker
staffArchetypes.purpose: Create structured document packages from approved templates, evidence, and task requirements.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: higher reasoning/writing model for final packages

staffArchetypes.label: Report Maker
staffArchetypes.purpose: Summarize department health, KPIs, blockers, staff workload, and next actions for human review.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: fast summarization model with deterministic structure

staffArchetypes.label: CRM / Data Operator
staffArchetypes.purpose: Read and update CRM/local records according to schema-aware rules and preserve an event trail.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: low-cost deterministic model for schema routing and validation

staffArchetypes.label: File / Upload QA
staffArchetypes.purpose: Check uploaded files, tender PDFs, screenshots, imports, and mapping evidence before other staff rely on them.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: vision/document-capable model when files are visual or scanned

staffArchetypes.label: SEO Analyzer
staffArchetypes.purpose: Analyze organic search performance, content opportunities, and SEO evidence across Search Console, Analytics, Ahrefs/Semrush-style data, GCP exports, and local transcripts; return a manager-ready recommendation with clear confidence and missing-data limits.
staffArchetypes.preferredModel: balanced SEO reasoning model by default; advanced model for multi-source diagnosis or strategy recommendations
requiresApprovalFor: creating public content without manager review
stopConditions: no active data source and no uploaded dataset
staffArchetypes.whenToUse: Use when a workflow stage needs keyword opportunity analysis, page performance diagnosis, SEO content prioritization, or a manager-ready SEO report.
staffArchetypes.defaultStageResponsibility: Read available SEO data, choose the strongest analysis template, explain which tools were active or missing, and return a structured recommendation.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: If missing, use uploaded CSV/text evidence or ask Manager to request Search Console setup.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Use the active Google Search Console connection assigned to the department organization account.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: If missing, produce search-only recommendations and mark engagement analysis as unavailable.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Use active Google Analytics or equivalent analytics connection when assigned.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: If Ahrefs/Semrush-style data is missing, avoid claiming keyword difficulty or backlink evidence.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Use active third-party SEO dataset/API when assigned; otherwise use internal/public evidence only.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: If GCP is missing, ask Manager for exported tables or continue with available SEO sources.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Use active GCP service account/project connection assigned in Settings -> Tools.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: If report rendering is unavailable, return structured markdown/JSON sections.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Use local report output unless the department assigns a document/dashboard lane.

requiredLanesTools.fallbackBehavior: If Keyword Planner is missing, use Search Console query evidence, uploaded exports, and public/internal research; do not claim paid-volume estimates.
requiredLanesTools.providerResolutionRule: Use active Google Ads Keyword Planner or equivalent lane when assigned.

staffArchetypes.missingDataBehavior: Produce a limited analysis only when at least one trusted data source or uploaded dataset is available; otherwise route a missing-input task to the AI Manager.

modelTiers.label: Cheap
modelTiers.useWhen: Single-source summaries, quick keyword lists, or data availability checks.

modelTiers.label: Balanced
modelTiers.useWhen: Normal SEO opportunity scoring and report drafting.

modelTiers.label: Advanced
modelTiers.useWhen: Multi-source diagnosis, strategy, conflicting data, or executive recommendations.

toolFallbackMatrix.behavior: Analyze queries, pages, impressions, clicks, CTR, and position; mark engagement and backlink conclusions as unavailable.
Combine search demand with engagement behavior and prioritize content refreshes by both acquisition and on-site signal.

toolFallbackMatrix.condition: Search Console, Analytics, and Ahrefs/Semrush active
toolFallbackMatrix.behavior: Add competition, keyword difficulty, backlinks, and gap evidence to the prioritization model.
Use only uploaded/exported data if provided; otherwise ask the AI Manager to request connection setup or source files.

instructionLayers.summary: Never pretend unavailable SEO tools are active; state data coverage and limits.
Use department tone, target market, site context, and approval policy.
Select one analysis template and required inputs for the project/task.
Choose cheap, balanced, or advanced reasoning based on budget and risk.

validationRules: At least one active connection or uploaded trusted dataset is required
Service accounts are referenced, not pasted into prompts
Missing tools must be listed in the output limitations
inputContract.missingInputAction: Ask AI Manager to request setup or source files.

outputContractDetail.approvalRequirement: Manager review before external report sharing or publishing action.

forbiddenBehavior: do not invent Search Console/Analytics data
do not expose service account secrets
do not change GCP/Search Console settings

retryableErrors: empty API page with valid property
retryPolicy.askManagerWhen: tool missing, permissions unclear, or confidence below acceptable threshold
retryPolicy.askHumanWhen: organization credentials, service account access, or publishing approval is needed
retryPolicy.createThreadWhen: approval, missing connection, missing dataset, or strategic review is required
retryPolicy.markWorkflowBlockedWhen: analysis cannot proceed with available data

versionLog.summary: Initial reusable SEO Analyzer template with model tiers, analysis templates, and connection fallback matrix.

platformSafetySkills.label: Supervised External Actions
platformSafetySkills.summary: External communication, tender submission, destructive data changes, and learning application require approved gates.
rules: Do not send external supplier/client emails without approval.
Do not submit tender portals automatically.
Do not delete CRM rows or files as normal staff work.
Do not apply learned behavior updates without human approval.

platformSafetySkills.label: Manager First Human Contact
platformSafetySkills.summary: Specialist AI Staff report to the AI Manager; only the Manager asks the human directly.
rules: Specialist staff report blockers/questions to AIstaff_Manager.
Create human-facing threads only for decisions, blockers, approvals, missing inputs, review, or escalation.

platformSafetySkills.label: No Backend/System Detail Disclosure
platformSafetySkills.summary: Keep provider keys, backend routing, raw operation keys, and protected system prompts out of user-facing workspace views.
rules: Do not expose provider keys or raw operation IDs in normal workspace views.
Do not show sponsored participants recipes, lanes, scoring internals, or staff implementation details.

departmentSkills.label: GCC Lab Tone And Vocabulary
departmentSkills.summary: Use GCC lab tender vocabulary, concise business tone, and approved manager/sponsor naming.
rules: Use tender Lead, tender case, supplier, quotation, package, and submission vocabulary.
Write concise business updates with exact blocker, next owner, and next action.
Accept English/Persian mixed instructions from Iman.

departmentSkills.label: GCC Lab Approval Policy
departmentSkills.summary: Department-level approval and visibility policy inherited by every staff member.
rules: Incomplete Leads are not closed automatically unless Iman approves an auto-close policy.
Human-facing questions are phrased as operational choices for Alex to route.

departmentSkills.label: Owner / Sponsored Visibility
departmentSkills.summary: Owners inspect department structure; sponsored participants see assigned work only.
rules: Owners/admins can inspect staff, skills, connections, databases, quality gates, and outputs.
Sponsored participants see tasks, uploads, confirmations, approvals, and accepted outputs only.

staffTemplateSkills.label: Manager Orchestrator Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Interpret human intent, route specialist work, consolidate results, and escalate only real decisions.
rules: Keep Iman informed through task threads.

staffTemplateSkills.label: Thinking Analyst Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Read evidence, reason over fit/risk, and produce structured recommendations with sources.

staffTemplateSkills.label: Outreach Operator Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Prepare professional business outreach, summarize replies, and keep send actions approval-gated.
rules: Ask Manager before sending or changing approved text.

staffTemplateSkills.label: Document Maker Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Create tender packages from templates, evidence, compliance requirements, and approved inputs.
rules: Keep package QA explicit before external send.

staffTemplateSkills.label: Report Maker Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Summarize department health, KPIs, blockers, workload, and next actions.
rules: Avoid hiding failed or waiting work.

staffTemplateSkills.label: CRM Operator Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Use schema-aware CRM/local data operations with event trails and guarded writes.

staffTemplateSkills.label: File QA Skill
staffTemplateSkills.summary: Validate tender files, uploads, screenshots, imports, and mapping evidence before downstream work uses them.

laneAdapterSkills.label: Email Queue Safety Lane
laneAdapterSkills.summary: Queue, preflight, and process approved outreach without bypassing content/attachment/duplicate checks.
rules: Draft before send when approval is required.

laneAdapterSkills.label: Gmail Adapter Skill
laneAdapterSkills.summary: Gmail-specific execution rules for drafts/sends, thread IDs, message IDs, and errors.
rules: Handle Gmail API/MCP errors without retry storms.

laneAdapterSkills.label: Outlook Adapter Skill
laneAdapterSkills.summary: Outlook/Microsoft Graph-specific rules for conversation IDs, attachments, sent item IDs, and errors.

laneAdapterSkills.label: Local CRM Adapter Skill
laneAdapterSkills.summary: Local task/thread/SQLite execution rules before optional CRM sync.
rules: Queue CRM sync actions instead of blocking local work.
Keep last error and evidence link.

operatingModel.label: Reusable Supervised AI Department Operating Model

departmentContract.goal: Each solution becomes a reusable supervised AI Department with an AI Manager, specialist AI Staff, project plans, quality gates, outputs, connections, databases, and reusable templates.
departmentContract.humanCommunicationRule: The human talks to the AI Manager. Specialist AI Staff work underneath the Manager.
departmentContract.staffCommunicationRule: Specialist AI Staff report to the AI Manager and do not ask the human directly unless the Manager routes the request.
departmentContract.managerRole: Interpret human intent, route specialist work, consolidate results, and escalate only real decisions or blockers.
departmentContract.safetyBoundary: External supplier emails, tender submissions, learning updates, destructive CRM changes, and incomplete-case closures remain supervised.

threadPolicy.closedThreadLearning: Closed threads may create skill-update candidates, but applying learnings requires human approval.

skillArchitecture.model: Scoped skills with explicit bindings, not one giant skill table.
skillArchitecture.overrideRule: User-editable preferences and learned updates must never override locked platform safety, department policy, or lane/tool execution rules.

automationRuntimeArchitecture.currentApproach: Use the platform database, explicit queue/state tables, cron triggers, and the internal runner before adding an external workflow engine.
runnerResponsibilities: stop at approval, missing input, or safety gates

projectSchedulerPolicy.goal: Every AI Department project should have durable scheduler state and a plan whose steps are owned by the AI Manager or specialist AI Staff.
projectSchedulerPolicy.ownerRule: Plan steps are assigned to AI Manager or specialist AI Staff; human-facing work is routed through Manager threads.
projectSchedulerPolicy.progressRule: Async work must write partial progress so operators can replay, triage, or resume without trusting in-process memory.

retryPolicy.operatorExpectation: Failures should leave enough state, last error, and next action for support triage.

externalWorkflowEnginePolicy.triggerDevDecision: Do not add Trigger.dev in v1.
externalWorkflowEnginePolicy.reason: The current database plus cron/internal-runner approach is enough while the product model still needs stronger plan, state, approval, visibility, and escalation discipline.
reconsiderWhen: step-level durable execution is needed across many autonomous jobs
long-running workflows need built-in heartbeats or cancellation
concurrency control is difficult to maintain locally
workflow replay UI becomes a product/support requirement
distributed locks are needed across multiple runners
deep observability is required across many departments

internalPlatformTeam.label: Designer Agent
internalPlatformTeam.purpose: Turn UI requests into approved patterns and acceptance checks before implementation.
internalPlatformTeam.laneRule: Design review happens before code edits for UI changes.

internalPlatformTeam.label: Upload QA Agent
internalPlatformTeam.purpose: Check screenshots, document references, imports, and mapping evidence before implementation relies on them.
internalPlatformTeam.laneRule: Upload evidence is verified before downstream implementation.

internalPlatformTeam.label: Codex Developer
internalPlatformTeam.purpose: Own code edits, validation, PR body, versioning, and final handoff.
internalPlatformTeam.laneRule: One actor owns one code branch or implementation lane at a time.

internalPlatformTeam.label: Deployment Watcher
internalPlatformTeam.purpose: Verify merged changes are actually live in the deployed runtime.
internalPlatformTeam.laneRule: Deployment verification is separate from implementation.

internalPlatformTeam.label: M3 UX Auditor
internalPlatformTeam.purpose: Check Material 3, house style, and page-pattern drift.
internalPlatformTeam.laneRule: UX drift is reported as review evidence, not hidden inside implementation notes.

internalPlatformTeam.label: Review Resolver
internalPlatformTeam.purpose: Check unresolved AI and human PR comments and route fixes.
internalPlatformTeam.laneRule: Review comments are resolved before final handoff.

workspaceBusinessProfile.industrySector: Testing, calibration, inspection, and tender services

compatibilityRules.label: Compatible With Local Alpha Runtime
compatibilityRules.summary: This object can run in the current local Python/React + SQLite alpha without production cloud orchestration.

compatibilityRules.label: Compatible With Local P4 Composition Resolver
compatibilityRules.summary: This skill pack can be resolved into the effective local P4 runtime context.
